title: 字母袖扣谋杀案
summary:
Following the news of a fourth murder, the hotel manager, Lazari, leads Poirot and Cattermole to Room 402 on the fifth floor of the Brawley's Hotel. Unlike the previous crime scenes, the victim, Jenny Hobbs, is missing. The room contains a large pool of blood and long bloodstains suggesting a body was dragged toward the door rather than the window.

In a corner of the room, Cattermole discovers Jenny Hobbs's deep brown hat, which contains a small piece of metal. Poirot identifies the object as a cufflink bearing the initials "PJI," a discovery that causes him significant emotional pain and self-reproach. While investigating the bloodstains, Poirot notes the width of the door, though Cattermole does not immediately grasp the significance of this detail.

Lazari recounts that Jenny Hobbs arrived at the hotel in a frantic state, appearing to be fleeing from someone. He personally escorted her to Room 402 after she demanded any available room. When Lazari returned with a master key thirty minutes later, he found the room in its current state. During the investigation, Cattermole looks out the window toward the hotel garden and observes the reception assistant, Thomas Brine, embracing a woman near a brown handcart. Poirot concludes the investigation of the room by questioning Lazari about whether any notes or room service requests were made regarding the fourth murder.
